name: Old-Time Potato Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a crock or large jar.
2. Allow the mixture to stand in a warm place, uncovered, for several days.
3. This is the author's last choice for making a starter and seems to be common in many sourdough cookbooks. Use it only as a last resort.
Note:
All containers used for starters that do not involve yeast must be carefully scalded before use. Failure to do so may result in an unsuccessful starter.
